notes: Low-power IA processor for CE, netbooks, smartphones. Moorestown (2010), Medfield (2012). Failed in smartphones; discontinued for mobile by 2016.
Entry-level desktop/notebook processor. Clarkdale family introduced Jan 2010.
High-end consumer processor. Core i7-980x delivered 20 GHz over 6 cores (12 threads) at time of writing.
Server/workstation processor family. Variants for datacenter, storage (Jasper Forest), communications.
Non-IA mission-critical processor. Not instruction-compatible with Xeon. RAS features migrated to Xeon. Last Itanium 9700 shipped 2021.
Intel-led mobile Linux distribution for CE/netbooks. Merged with Nokia MeeGo in Feb 2010. Ultimately superseded by Tizen.
Merger of Intel Moblin and Nokia Maemo. Only one phone shipped (Nokia N9). Succeeded by Tizen (Samsung/Intel) and Sailfish OS (Jolla).
Intel invested hundreds of millions in 2003-2005 to accelerate WiFi infrastructure adoption globally.
High-speed serial interconnect standard. Implicit in datacenter/server architecture discussion.
Many-core IA GPU project. Redirected from discrete graphics to HPC in 2009. Cancelled as product; technology evolved into Xeon Phi (Knights Corner/Landing).
R&D project for many-core IA chips. 48-core prototype demonstrated Dec 2009. Technology concepts fed into Xeon Phi.
High-end Xeon with Itanium-derived RAS features. Shared common I/O chipset with Itanium.
32nm shrink of Nehalem architecture (tick in tick-tock). Introduced Jan 2010 with Clarkdale (desktop) and Arrandale (notebook).
Enterprise management and security platform. Fortune 500 adoption underway; next target was SMB market.
Intel investing to be leader in enterprise and consumer SSDs. Paper predicted rapid datacenter adoption by mid-decade.
High-performance interconnect for HPC clusters. Implicit in Intel's Top 500 supercomputer dominance discussion.
Paper describes cloud as 'big hype trend' with hazy definitions but real merit. Salesforce.com cited as example. Intel extending Xeon to support massive cloud workloads.
